title: phanera lingua var. antipolana
intro: Phanera lingua var. antipolana, also known as the Philippine bamboo orchid, is a species of epiphytic orchid native to the Philippines. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, making it a valuable remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for use in traditional medicine, although modern research on its efficacy and safety is limited.
